prompt: This loop builds a large string very slowly due to a performance problem. What is the issue?
choices: String concatenation with + is not allowed inside loops
Strings are immutable, so result + word creates a new object every iteration, causing O(n²) memory allocations
The loop variable i should start at 1 to avoid an empty first concatenation
result must be initialized to word, not empty string
explanation: Each += on String creates a new String object (Strings are immutable). For n iterations, this allocates n objects and copies O(1+2+...+n)=O(n²) characters total. Fix: use StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); sb.append(word) in the loop; return sb.toString().
